ground_truth_answer: The athlete exhibits severe bilateral asymmetries in both knee (26.6° difference) and hip (29.9° difference) angles, with the right side achieving significantly greater flexion than the left. This requires unilateral corrective exercises focusing on left-side mobility and strength, motor control drills for symmetry, and injury risk mitigation strategies.
